title: Refactor a GenServer handle_call that does defensive nil-munging
prompt:
Given:

```
def handle_call({:get, key}, _from, state) do
  if state == nil do
    {:reply, {:error, :no_state}, state}
  else
    value = state[key]
    if is_nil(value) do
      {:reply, {:error, :not_found}, state}
    else
      {:reply, {:ok, value}, state}
    end
  end
end
```

Rewrite as three handle_call clauses: one matching `{:get, _key}, _from, nil`, one matching `{:get, key}, _from, state` with a `Map.fetch(state, key)` via `case`, and a fallback. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.KV do ... end` with just the handle_call clauses and `use GenServer`.
tier_rationale: Multi-clause handle_call refactor + assertive Map.fetch. Sonnet typically leaves the nil state check in the body.
